findings.title: Catastrophic cascade potential: OrchestratorAgent failure cascades to 12 agents
findings.description: Agent 'OrchestratorAgent' (id=6d97b13c-adb0-4a22-827c-fba1facdbd83) has a blast radius of 92.3% — failure would directly or indirectly impact 12 of 14 agents.
findings.remediation: Introduce circuit breakers, health checks, and fallback agents to isolate failures. Consider replicating this agent.

findings.title: Unusual delegation path: ImageValidatorAgent → OrchestratorAgent
findings.description: Peripheral agent 'ImageValidatorAgent' (centrality=0.00) delegates to highly central agent 'OrchestratorAgent' (centrality=0.89). This pattern may indicate privilege escalation or intent drift.
findings.remediation: Review delegation patterns. Ensure low-privilege agents cannot invoke high-privilege orchestrator agents without authorization.

findings.title: Dense communication clique detected (3 agents)
findings.description: Agents ['OrchestratorAgent', 'TrainerAgent', 'EvolutionAgent'] form a fully-connected communication clique. This dense subgraph may indicate coordinated behaviour or information sharing outside the orchestrator's oversight.
findings.remediation: Audit communication logs for this agent cluster. Enforce hub-and-spoke topology via an orchestrator agent. Add rate limiting on peer-to-peer agent communication.

findings.title: Dense communication clique detected (3 agents)
findings.description: Agents ['OrchestratorAgent', 'FileOptimizerAgent', 'DocumentProcessorAgent'] form a fully-connected communication clique. This dense subgraph may indicate coordinated behaviour or information sharing outside the orchestrator's oversight.
findings.remediation: Audit communication logs for this agent cluster. Enforce hub-and-spoke topology via an orchestrator agent. Add rate limiting on peer-to-peer agent communication.

findings.title: Dense communication clique detected (3 agents)
findings.description: Agents ['OrchestratorAgent', 'FileOptimizerAgent', 'PrintOptimizerAgent'] form a fully-connected communication clique. This dense subgraph may indicate coordinated behaviour or information sharing outside the orchestrator's oversight.
findings.remediation: Audit communication logs for this agent cluster. Enforce hub-and-spoke topology via an orchestrator agent. Add rate limiting on peer-to-peer agent communication.

findings.title: Orchestrator-bypass cycle: DocumentProcessorAgent → FileOptimizerAgent
findings.description: Agents ['DocumentProcessorAgent', 'FileOptimizerAgent'] form a cyclic dependency that completely bypasses any orchestrator or manager agent. This allows coordinated actions without oversight.
findings.remediation: Redesign the agent graph so all cycles pass through an orchestrator node that can audit decisions.

findings.title: Single Point of Failure: OrchestratorAgent
findings.description: Agent 'OrchestratorAgent' is an articulation point — removing it would disconnect the agent communication graph. Blast radius: 92.3% of agents affected.
findings.remediation: Introduce redundant agents or fallback paths. Consider load balancing across multiple agents for this role. Implement circuit breakers on connections to this agent.

findings.title: No timeout handling evidence: OrchestratorAgent
findings.description: Agent 'OrchestratorAgent' has 14 upstream and 14 downstream connections but no recorded timeout or error events, suggesting it may not handle slow responses gracefully.
findings.remediation: Implement timeout handling and error propagation. Agents should emit TIMEOUT or ERROR events when upstream calls exceed acceptable latency.

findings.title: Fragile dependency: ImageValidatorAgent relies solely on OrchestratorAgent
findings.description: Agent 'ImageValidatorAgent' has a single upstream dependency on 'OrchestratorAgent' with no timeout or error events recorded. If 'OrchestratorAgent' responds slowly or fails, 'ImageValidatorAgent' has no fallback.
findings.remediation: Add redundant upstream agents or implement a timeout with a default/cached response when the sole upstream is slow.
